name: Multiple Database Failures Followed by Success
notes: Responds when there are multiple database failures followed by a success within a short period of time.
bb_references: BB:Database: System Action Allow in any order with the same IP from the previous source
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:HostDefinition: Database Servers, BB:HostReference: Database Servers

when at least 50 of these BB:Database: System Action Deny, in any order, with the same source IP followed by at least 1 of these BB:Database: System Action Allow in any order with the same IP from the previous source, within 5 minutes
thresholds: when at least 50 of these BB:Database: System Action Deny, in any order, with the same source IP followed by at least 1 of these BB:Database: System Action Allow in any order with the same IP from the previous source, within 5 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Multiple Database Failures Followed by Success on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:HostDefinition: Database Servers, BB:HostReference: Database Servers

AND when at least 50 of these BB:Database: System Action Deny, in any order, with the same source IP followed by at least 1 of these BB:Database: System Action Allow in any order with the same IP from the previous source, within 5 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Database failures followed by success
Event description: Responds when there are multiple database failures followed by a success within a short period of time.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
